untranslatable_reason: The pattern requires visual identification of two distinct bumps (peaks) with the first being pointed/narrow, and timing of the reversal relative to peak formation. These multi-bar pattern structures and morphological characteristics (pointed shape, narrow width) cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators and comparison operators. Would require pattern recognition logic or look-back window analysis not supported by the current indicator set.
translation_notes: Attempted to approximate using price action (higher high followed by close below open as reversal signal), but this is a simplified proxy. The true Bump-and-Run Reversal Top pattern from Bulkowski requires identifying: (1) first pointed peak formation, (2) second peak formation, (3) specific timing/spacing between peaks, and (4) the subsequent downward reversal. The 70% average decline parameter and minimal exit time window are documented but cannot be directly encoded in condition rules.
